title: Slop, Business Impact & Owning It
goal: Spot slop, apply AI to real work, and measure what you ship.

blocks.html: Slop is AI output that looks fine but says nothing — or is subtly wrong. Spotting it is the difference between AI helping you and AI embarrassing you.

blocks.question: Which of these is a sign of AI slop?

options.text: Generic filler that could apply to any company and says nothing specific.
options.explain: That's the tell. Slop is polished, confident, and empty.

options.text: A specific claim backed by a real number you can check.
options.explain: That's the opposite of slop — that's substance.

options.text: A short, plain answer to your exact question.
options.explain: Plain and direct is good, not slop.

options.text: Output you can verify against your real data.
options.explain: Verifiable output is exactly what you want.

lessons.title: Apply AI to One Business Task

blocks.html: Pick one real task and set AI up to do it — with a clear finish line. Build it and save it.

blocks.title: Apply-AI-to-a-task prompt
blocks.template: Help me {task}. Here's my situation: {context}. I'll know it's done well when {done}.

fields.label: Your situation
fields.placeholder: small coffee brand, casual voice, one new product

fields.label: Done well when
fields.placeholder: it's under 150 words and sounds like us

blocks.html: AI is only worth it if it moves a real number. Pick one and commit to tracking it honestly.

items.text: I picked ONE honest number this should improve (hours saved, replies sent…).
I know what that number was BEFORE (the baseline).
I'll check the number again in a couple of weeks.
If it didn't move, I'll change it or drop it — no pretending.

blocks.html: You built an AI helper that drafts your customer replies. It's working. What now?

start.text: It works today. What's the right next move?

choices.label: Track how much time it saves, and tweak the prompt as you learn
Set it to auto-send replies with no review
Forget about it and never check it again

own.text: You track the time saved and keep improving it.
own.outcome: That's ownership. You measure the real gain and keep it sharp. That's the whole game — owned, measured, improving.

auto.text: You let it auto-send with no review.
auto.outcome: Risky. Auto-sending customer replies with no human check is how one bad AI message reaches a real customer. Keep the approval step.

forget.text: You set it and forget it.
forget.outcome: Careful — models and your data drift over time. What works today can quietly break. Check in now and then.
